frameworks.name: Continuity Fields
frameworks.one_line: perceptual attraction toward recent inputs with temporal, feature, and spatial tuning

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: history-driven perceptual bias extended to clinical-style visual search

frameworks.name: medical image perception
frameworks.one_line: applied domain where uncorrelated images are sequentially classified

core_question: Does serial dependence bias shape classification during a clinically-inspired visual search task?
key_assumptions: successive search images are objectively uncorrelated
shape classification should be independent of recent visual experience
Continuity Fields generalize from simple stimuli to tumor-like shapes
manipulations: feature similarity between current and prior shapes
spatial separation between current and prior shape locations
key_findings: classification errors biased ~7% toward the previous image's shape
attraction effect extended ~12 seconds back in time
effect occurred only between similar tumor-like shapes
effect occurred only within a limited spatial region
interpretations: serial dependence shapes perception during visual search, not only simple tasks
history-driven attraction may detrimentally affect clinical image interpretation
